methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Late Stage → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $2.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 17 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $400M - $7.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 53.2x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Vast is a late-stage Space & Aerospace company founded by Jake Cannell and Christian Horne, currently valued at $2.0B post-money on $300M+ in total raised capital. The valuation analysis benchmarks against a 17-company peer universe spanning both public and private companies in the same sector, including Planet Labs ($7.0B), Firefly Aerospace ($6.3B), Relativity Space ($4.2B), Astranis ($3.5B), and ICEYE ($2.8B).

Two valuation methods prove applicable to this stage. Stage-progression analysis, applying a typical 1.2x late-stage step-up multiplier to the $2.0B last post-money valuation, implies $2.4B. Peer-median benchmarking across 17 same-sector comparable companies yields a median valuation of $1.3B, with a range spanning $400M to $7.0B. Public-comp P/S anchoring cannot be applied due to absence of target revenue disclosure, which is standard for private companies. The sector-median P/S across six public Space & Aerospace tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) is 53.2x, but lacks a revenue input for Vast.

The triangulated analysis of the two applicable methods supports a valuation range of $1.1B (low) to $2.8B (high), with a median of $1.9B. The peer-median result sits notably below the current $2.0B valuation, while stage-progression slightly exceeds it. Key uncertainties include whether Vast's revenue trajectory and margins justify a premium to sector peers and the timing of any next financing event.
